summary: Review of changes addressing gndrmnn's review #5 feedback. All 7 code comments were addressed correctly: log level downgraded from Info to Debug via V(1), useless test removed (TestReconcile_ReadyFalseWhenSubConditionMissing tested Go struct assignment, not controller logic), custom findCondition helper replaced with meta.FindStatusCondition across 6 test files, config.MergeDefaults second argument changed to defaults for clarity, custom boolPtr replaced with ptr.To from k8s.io/utils/ptr, and CRD YAML regenerated after API struct changes. All changes are minimal, correct, and directly responsive to reviewer feedback.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (go test ./operators/keystone/... — all pass)
Edge cases are covered (existing tests unchanged, only removed a test that verified Go language semantics)
No test coverage regression (removed test did not exercise production code)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (meta.FindStatusCondition, ptr.To from upstream packages)
No code duplication (custom helpers removed in favor of standard library)
Clear naming (defaults argument communicates intent better than merged)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible (uses standard library functions instead of custom helpers)
Changes scoped exactly to reviewer feedback — no scope creep

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code (findCondition and boolPtr removed, 0 remaining occurrences)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Errors detected early (no changes to error handling)

defensive_checklist.item: All external inputs validated (no changes to validation logic)

issues_found.description: Stub scanner flagged TODO comment at line 160. This is a false positive — the TODO documents a deliberate feature deferral (credential key management) with a working workaround (Optional: ptr.To(true)). Credential key management is explicitly listed in the Excluded scope. No action required.
issues_found.fix: None — the TODO is appropriate documentation of a known, scoped deferral.

title: F001: Implement Keystone reconciler and sub-reconcilers
